settings.heading: Your Go-To for the Easiest, Most Iconic Piercing
settings.subheading:
Lobe piercings are the most common types of piercings worldwide. Spanning across different cultures and countries, lobe piercings are also a popular choice for many people of all ages.

JEWELLERY: Typically, butterfly-back studs, or small captive bead rings/hoops.
Titanium, 14K Gold, or 316L Surgical Steel (hypoallergenic options).

STANDARD SIZE: 18G (1.0 mm) or 20G (0.8 mm)

DOWNSIZE: 6–8 weeks, once initial swelling subsides. Opt for a shorter post to avoid snagging and help with proper healing.

HEALING TIME: 6–8 weeks for most people as the tissue is very soft and thin in this area.

PAIN LEVEL: Mild (2–3/10) where the piercing itself is a quick pinch.

AFTERCARE: Clean twice daily with a sterile saline solution (store-bought or homemade).
Avoid twisting and touching the jewellery and keep hair away from the piercing to prevent irritation and snagging. Always use a saline solution for aftercare and cleaning.

PRO-TIP: Try not to wear heavy jewellery initially or after the piercing has just healed. Lightweight jewellery in the beginning is best for the piercing and ensures the pierced hole lasts long without stretching too much.

settings.heading: Upper Lobe Piercings
settings.subheading:
Upper lobe piercings are becoming increasingly popular amongst people who want to opt for more than just the standard lobe but don't want to commit to piercing their cartilage area. This option is great in creating that stacked look with minimal pressure.

JEWELLERY: Flatback Studs, Captive Bead Rings, Circular Barbells
Titanium, 14K Gold, or 316L Surgical Steel (hypoallergenic options).

STANDARD SIZE: 18G (1.0 mm) or 20G (0.8 mm)

DOWNSIZE: 6–8 weeks, once initial swelling subsides. Opt for a shorter post to avoid snagging and help with proper healing.

HEALING TIME: 6–8 weeks for most people as the tissue is very soft and thin in this area.

PAIN LEVEL: Moderate (3-4/10) due to tissue being slightly thicker than the lobe.

AFTERCARE: Clean twice daily with a sterile saline solution (store-bought or homemade).
Avoid twisting and touching the jewellery and keep hair away from the piercing to prevent irritation and snagging. Always use a saline solution for aftercare and cleaning.

PRO-TRIP: Avoid getting the upper lobe pierced at the same time as the standard lobe, allowing each piercing to heal individually before going onto the next.
